paper_title: Glycerol Dialkyl Glycerol Tetraether Extraction and Age Model U1356

methods.description: This method describes the process of extracting and analyzing glycerol dialkyl glycerol tetraethers (GDGTs) from sediment samples to determine TEX86 values, which are used as a proxy for past sea surface temperatures.

steps.description: Lipids were extracted through accelerated solvent extraction (ASE) with a dichloromethane (DCM) / methanol (MeOH) mixture, 9:1 v/v, at 100°C and 7.6 × 10^6 Pa.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ASE lipid extraction using DCM/MeOH

steps.description: The lipid extract was separated using Al2O3 column chromatography and hexane/DCM (9:1, v/v), hexane/DCM (1:1, v/v), and DCM/MeOH (1:1, v/v) for separating apolar, ketone, and polar fractions, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: separate lipid extract using column chromatography

steps.description: 99 ng of C46 internal standard was added to the polar fraction for quantification purposes.
steps.searchable_summary: add C46 internal standard to polar fraction

steps.description: The polar fraction of each sample was dried under N2, dissolved in hexane/isopropanol (99:1, v/v), and filtered through a 0.45 µm 4 mm diameter polytetrafluorethylene filter.
steps.searchable_summary: dry, dissolve, and filter polar fraction

steps.description: The dissolved polar fractions were injected and analyzed by high-performance liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (HPLC-MS) or ultra-high-performance liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (UHPLC-MS).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze samples using HPLC-MS or UHPLC-MS

steps.description: GDGT peaks in the (U)HPLC chromatograms were integrated using ChemStation software.
Samples with too low concentrations of GDGTs were discarded.
steps.searchable_summary: discard samples with low GDGT concentrations

steps.description: The branched and isoprenoid tetraether (BIT) index was used to verify the relative contribution of terrestrial GDGTs.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate BIT index for terrestrial GDGT contribution

steps.description: Calculated methane index (MI), GDGT-0 / crenarchaeol, GDGT-2 / crenarchaeol ratios, and ring index to check for input of methanogenic or methanotrophic archaea.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate MI, GDGT ratios, and ring index

methods.description: This method outlines the construction of an age model for Oligocene sediments at IODP Hole U1356A using biostratigraphy, magnetostratigraphy, and constrained optimization methodology (CONOP).

steps.description: Oligocene sediments were recovered from 894.68 m b.s.f. to 432.64 m b.s.f. at IODP Hole U1356A.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve sediment data from IODP Hole U1356A

steps.description: The shipboard age model was based on biostratigraphy with magnetostratigraphic tie points and calibrated to the Geologic Time Scale of 2004.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate age model using biostratigraphy and magnetostratigraphy

steps.description: Recalibrated the existing age tie points to the Geologic Timescale of 2012.
steps.searchable_summary: recalibrate age tie points to GTS2012

steps.description: Converted data to the time domain using FO and LO of specific species and paleomagnetic tie points.
steps.searchable_summary: convert sediment data to time domain

steps.description: Applied constrained optimization methodology (CONOP) to diatom and radiolarian biostratigraphic events to construct an age model.
Identified hiatuses in the sediment record using the CONOP model.
